contract_for: Person B Query MCP -> Person C demo surface

query.text: Does activating OR51E2 / PSGR suppress or promote prostate cancer phenotypes? Summarize the evidence for LNCaP / prostate models, including β-ionone studies.

consensus.claim: Screened four candidate ligands (methylmalonic acid, 2,2-difluorocyclopropane-1-carboxylate, 3,3-difluoropropanoic acid, 4,4-difluoropentanoic acid) against a propionic acid positive control for OR51E2 activation, using a CREB-driven firefly luciferase / constitutive NanoLuc dual-reporter assay in transiently transfected HEK293 cells (pCI-OR51E2 + pGL4.53[luc2/PGK] + pCRE-Luc), at 0.5M and 1M ligand concentrations.

source.label: Bansal S, Gudisay S (2025). Dual-luciferase reporter assay data, unpublished (ISEF 2025 CB

consensus.model: HEK293 cells, transient transfection (heterologous OR51E2 expression)

tumor_suppressive.claim: Activation of OR51E2 (PSGR) by androstenone derivatives or beta-ionone inhibits proliferation of prostate cancer cells.

source.label: Neuhaus EM, Zhang W, Gelis L, Deng Y, Noldus J, Hatt H (2009). "Activation of an olfactory

tumor_suppressive.model: LNCaP cells (endogenous receptor); also HEK293 and PC-3 cells, and primary prostate epithelial cells

tumor_suppressive.claim: Inducible OR51E1/OR51E2 expression in LNCaP cells suppresses proliferation and promotes cell death; the paper explicitly flags field-wide controversy over whether beta-ionone is a genuine OR51E2 agonist.

source.label: Pronin A, Slepak V (2021). "Ectopically expressed olfactory receptors OR51E1 and OR51E2 su

tumor_suppressive.claim: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout of OR51E2 in LNCaP cells INCREASES proliferation, migration, adhesion, anchorage-independent colony formation, and tumor growth -- i.e. loss of OR51E2 produces a more aggressive phenotype. In the TCGA PRAD cohort, low OR51E2 expression correlates with worse prognosis and higher Gleason grade.

source.label: Thomsen ARB, et al. (2025). "CRISPR-Cas9 knockout of OR51E2 in prostate cancer cells revea

tumor_suppressive.model: LNCaP cells (CRISPR-Cas9 knockout); cross-referenced against TCGA-PRAD cohort
tumor_suppressive.endpoint: proliferation, migration, tumor growth, prognosis correlation

tumor_promoting.claim: Beta-ionone stimulation of PSGR/OR51E2 promotes LNCaP cell invasiveness; co-administration of alpha-ionone (a PSGR antagonist) abrogates this effect.

source.label: Sanz G, Leray I, Dewaele A, Sobilo J, Lerondel S, Bouet S, Grebert D, Monnerie R, Pajot-Au

tumor_promoting.claim: PSGR (OR51E2) overexpression drives premalignant prostate lesions and larger xenograft tumors.

source.label: Rodriguez M, Luo W, Weng J, Zeng L, Yi Z, Siwko S, Liu M (2014). "PSGR promotes prostatic

tumor_promoting.model: PSGR-transgenic mouse model (probasin promoter); LNCaP xenograft (mouse)

tumor_promoting.claim: Alpha-ionone, previously characterized (Sanz 2014) as blocking beta-ionone's pro-invasive effect, is actually a full OR51E2 agonist in its own right that promotes tumor GROWTH (not invasion) via a distinct pathway from beta-ionone. Combined alpha+beta-ionone treatment increases total tumor burden more than either ligand alone.

source.label: Sanz G, Le Poupon C, Bouet S, et al. (2016). "Alpha-ionone is a full agonist of the prosta

tumor_promoting.claim: In the tumor microenvironment (TME), palmitic acid binds OR51E2 on tumor-associated macrophages (TAMs) — NOT tumor cells — and enhances their pro-tumor phenotype. In vivo deletion of OR51E2 (and other chemosensors) in TAMs caused cancer regression and increased CD8+ T cell infiltration.

source.label: Marelli G, Morina N, Portale F, et al. (2025). "Palmitic acid activates OR51E2 on tumor-as

tumor_promoting.model: Human prostate tissue / mouse TAM-specific OR51E2 deletion model

exploratory.claim: Discloses 24 novel OR51E2 agonists (e.g. 19-OH AD, AFMK, glycyl-glycine, kojibiose, estriol, pelargonidin, palmitic acid) and 1 antagonist (13-cis retinoic acid/isotretinoin), claiming methods to treat/prevent castrate-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) and diagnose prostate cancer via metabolite biomarkers, based on the premise that chronic agonist-driven OR51E2 activation drives neuroendocrine trans-differentiation.

source.label: US10588884B2, "Modulators of prostate-specific G-protein receptor (PSGR/OR51E2) and method

exploratory.model: Patent disclosure (in vitro/in vivo data referenced in specification, not independently verified here)

exploratory.claim: Divisional continuation of US10588884B2 covering the same 24 agonists/1 antagonist, expanding claims to metabolomic-profiling diagnostic methods and describing a proposed link between Propionibacterium acnes infection and prostate cancer progression via OR51E2 activation.

source.label: US12186294B2, "Modulators of prostate-specific g-protein receptor (PSGR/OR51E2) and method

exploratory.model: Patent disclosure (in vitro/in vivo data referenced in specification, not independently verified here)

tensions.title: OR51E2 activation outcome in prostate cancer is contested
tensions.summary: CONFIRMED DIFFERENT ENDPOINTS: suppressive evidence measures proliferation, proliferation, migration, tumor growth, prognosis correlation; promoting evidence measures immune evasion / TAM polarization (non-cell-autonomous), invasiveness, tumor growth (in vivo), tumor_growth. This is contested on overall clinical/therapeutic implication, but not a strict same-endpoint contradiction — both effects could be simultaneously true (e.g. reduced proliferation alongside increased invasiveness is a recognized cancer biology pattern). Do not present as a flat two-sided contradiction; present as divergent evidence on distinct outcomes. Additionally: Note from Neuhaus EM: Cross-checked against CLAUDE.md Section 6 table and the friend's (Bansal/Gudisay) Literature_Review.docx summary, which  | Note from Pronin A: Confirmed via PubMed/PMC (PMC8024707) and J Biol Chem fulltext -- ligand-specificity finding and explicit controversy st

weight_breakdown.note: Individual weights sum to the mass totals above. Patents are tracked separately (commercial_interest only) and excluded from mass.

rules.text: OR51E2 activation appears to suppress tumor growth (supported by 3 independent sources)
rules.qualification: Contested -- do not treat as settled

rules.text: OR51E2 activation appears to promote tumor growth or invasiveness (supported by 6 independent sources)
rules.qualification: Contested -- do not treat as settled

adjudication.reason: Support and oppose masses are within balance threshold on the same receptor/cell-line family.

elicitation.message: OR51E2 activation evidence is balanced between tumor-suppressive and tumor-promoting claims. How should we proceed?

options.label: Keep as contested -- do not merge into one rule

fallback_without_elicitation.instruction: If MCP elicitation is unavailable, return this object and wait for the researcher's next message selecting an option id.

baseline_contrast.plain_k_pro_expected: A smoothed or hedged single narrative that the receptor may have context-dependent effects, without explicitly staging the primary-literature split as a first-class tension.
baseline_contrast.augmented_expected: Explicit contested cluster (Neuhaus EM, Pronin A, Thomsen ARB vs. Marelli G, Rodriguez M, Sanz G, US10588884B2, US12186294B2) with sources, weights, endpoint labels, confidence-qualified rules, and needs_judgment elicitation.
